openagent.description: spec version, e.g. "0.2". Optional pre-1.0 (validators warn when missing); moves into 'required' at spec 1.0. Always set it.

recipe.description: optional regeneration recipe — makes the canonical likeness reproducible (model + prompt + seed) the same way voice is from base + style, instead of a single frozen PNG.

model.description: the named image model that produced ref (e.g. "imagen-4", "flux-1.1-pro").

prompt.description: the generation prompt that yields the canonical likeness.

seed.description: the seed used; pin it for deterministic re-gens (omit if the model exposes none).

links.description: optional external links. A free-form string map; a few keys are conventional. `agent_card` is the bridge to capability specs: it points to this agent's A2A AgentCard (or equivalent), which describes what the agent can DO — OpenAgent describes who it IS, so the two compose instead of competing.

profile.description: the agent's public profile / home page.

agent_card.description: URL of this agent's A2A AgentCard (or equivalent capability descriptor) — the machine-readable contract of what the agent can DO. OpenAgent is the identity/persona layer ON TOP of that capability layer; linking the card composes the two specs.

provenance.description: optional per-file authorship, integrity proof, and remix lineage (spec v0.2). Additive — absence is fully valid v0.1.

created_by.description: the authoring identity. The ed25519 'key' IS the identity; 'name'/'url' are human labels for it.

key.description: ed25519 public key (PEM block or bare base64 SPKI) the signature verifies against.

url.description: optional link to the author (profile, repo, site).

signed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the signature was produced; covered by the signature.

derived_from.description: remix lineage — the parent persona(s) this one was forked/remixed from. Each edge is one ancestor.

source.description: where the parent file lives (URL or path), so the lineage is resolvable.

relation.description: how this persona relates to the parent. Defaults to 'fork' if omitted.

signature.description: optional: the parent's provenance.signature at fork time, pinning the exact revision forked.
detached ed25519 signature (base64) over the canonical persona document with this field removed. Proves integrity + key-authorship.

ext.description: sanctioned extension namespace (spec v0.2) — the escape hatch for tool-specific data. The core spec is closed (additionalProperties:false everywhere); adopters put their own fields UNDER ext instead of forking the schema. Each key is a tool/vendor namespace and its value is that tool's free-form object, so two tools never collide. The core spec ignores ext, and it does not affect computed rarity; it IS covered by provenance signing as part of the document.
